page: Amazon Web Services r7g.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by custom AWS Graviton3 processors using the ARM64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated physical cores running at 2.6 GHz on the Neoverse-V1 microarchitecture, with no hyperthreading. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio, or 8.0 GB per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and includes a 15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, but does not feature local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data indicates highly competitive memory performance, with the Passmark memory score reaching the top 10% tier. Large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidths both rank in the top 25%, highlighting efficient data throughput. Compute performance is solid, with Geekbench multi-core, Passmark CPU physics, and Passmark database operations scoring in the top 25%. However, single-core execution, cryptography, and complex vector operations sit in the middle 50% tier. LLM inference tasks show average to poor performance, particularly for large 70B models, reflecting the lack of dedicated hardware acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture and dedicated core allocation deliver high memory density and strong multi-threaded performance without the overhead of traditional x86 licensing. This makes the r7g.8xlarge highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads such as high-throughput database operations, in-memory caching, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing. It is less optimal for workloads requiring local high-speed storage, heavy single-threaded execution, or GPU-accelerated machine learning.
description: Amazon Web Services r7g.8xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server powered by 32 dedicated AWS Graviton3 physical cores running at 2.6 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio, alongside a 15 Gbps baseline network speed and no local storage or GPUs. Benchmark results show top-tier memory performance, with Passmark memory scores in the top 10% and large-block memory bandwidth in the top 25%. Multi-core compute and database operations also perform strongly. This instance delivers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound applications due to its efficient ARM architecture and dedicated resource allocation. It is best suited for open-source databases, in-memory caches, static web serving, and scale-out enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7g.8xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server with 32 dedicated Graviton3 cores, 256 GB RAM, and 15 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory performance for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7g.8xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated Graviton3 cores, 256 GB RAM, and 15 Gbps network speed.
tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated cores and high-bandwidth memory for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 32 dedicated AWS Graviton3 ARM64 cores at 2.6 GHz
256 GB of system memory delivering top-tier bandwidth and throughput
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Excellent fit for in-memory databases, caching, and web serving
